Politics.headline: Trump Mocks Japan About Pearl Harbor in Response to Question About Iran War
Politics.detail: During a meeting with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, President Trump referenced Pearl Harbor when asked why allies weren't informed of the Iran plan, stating Japan was 'stepping up to the plate' to support the US war.

Politics.headline: Starmer plans to ease impact of immigration policy changes after backlash from Labour MPs
Politics.detail: Keir Starmer is reportedly planning to adjust the impact of proposed immigration policy changes following criticism from within the Labour party.

Politics.headline: US states sue Trump EPA over decision to repeal bedrock climate finding
Politics.detail: Several US states are suing the Trump administration's Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) over its decision to repeal a key climate finding.

Politics.headline: Trump references Pearl Harbor during meeting with Japanese PM on Iran war
Politics.detail: US President Donald Trump alluded to Pearl Harbor during a meeting with the Japanese Prime Minister regarding the Iran war, remarking that Japan was 'stepping up to the plate' to support the US effort.

Politics.headline: Poilievre says he used Joe Rogan podcast to argue against U.S. tariffs and 'fight for Canada'
Politics.detail: Canadian politician Pierre Poilievre stated that he utilized his appearance on the Joe Rogan podcast to advocate against U.S. tariffs and champion Canadian interests.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks close in red as oil sees sharp fluctuations
Finance & Economy.detail: US stocks closed Thursday with losses as oil prices saw sharp fluctuations amid the war in the Middle East, while European central banks kept policy rates unchanged. The Dow fell 0.44%, the Nasdaq declined 0.28%, and the S&P 500 slipped 0.27%.

Finance & Economy.headline: Japan approves bill to support overseas projects that can boost economic security
Finance & Economy.detail: The government approved a bill to create new ways to provide financial support to Japanese companies' overseas projects critical to Japan's economic security. The bill revises the economic security promotion law to attract private-sector funds by having the government assume the risk of losses.

Finance & Economy.headline: Proactive fiscal policy seen in 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: China will continue its proactive fiscal policy in 2026, using increased expenditure, a wider deficit, and government bond issuance to stimulate domestic demand and ensure stable economic growth. The budget deficit target remains at 4% of GDP, with a record high general public budget expenditure projected.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gold Plunges 5% as Central Banks Turn Hawkish
Finance & Economy.detail: Gold prices crashed over 5% to $4,560 per ounce as investors recalibrated rate expectations following hawkish central bank decisions and soaring energy prices amid the escalating Iran war. Markets have pushed back expectations for Fed rate cuts to 2027 and are pricing in rate hikes from the ECB and BOE this year.

Finance & Economy.headline: Canada, allies ready to help unblock Strait of Hormuz as oil prices spike
Finance & Economy.detail: Canada and its allies are prepared to assist in unblocking the Strait of Hormuz following a spike in oil prices. This action comes in response to escalating tensions and disruptions in the region that are impacting global oil supplies.

Technology.headline: Broadcom hit with EU antitrust complaint and request for interim measure
Technology.detail: Broadcom is facing an EU antitrust complaint regarding VMware partner practices, potentially leading to interim measures.

Technology.headline: Apple fends off bid for new Apple Watch import ban at US trade tribunal
Technology.detail: Apple has successfully defended against a new attempt to ban Apple Watch imports at a US trade tribunal.

Technology.headline: D2C food brand Anveshan eyes Rs 150-200 crore funding; valuation may more than double
Technology.detail: D2C food brand Anveshan is in discussions to raise Rs 150-200 crore, which could more than double its valuation.

Technology.headline: Cloud industry group calls for EU interim measure against Broadcom over VMware
Technology.detail: A cloud industry group is urging the EU to implement interim measures against Broadcom concerning its VMware practices.

Technology.headline: PhysicsWallah expands into the student hostel segment
Technology.detail: Edtech platform PhysicsWallah is expanding its services to include student hostels.

Science.headline: Earth's continental plates were moving 3.48 billion years ago
Science.detail: New research indicates that Earth's continental plates were already in motion 3.48 billion years ago, reshaping our understanding of early Earth dynamics. This finding sheds light on the planet's geological evolution and the processes that have shaped its surface over millennia.

Science.headline: New subatomic particle discovered at CERN's Large Hadron Collider
Science.detail: A new subatomic particle known as the Ξcc⁺, containing two charm quarks, has been discovered at CERN’s Large Hadron Collider. This discovery was made using the upgraded LHCb detector.

Science.headline: AI blurs line between real, fake content
Science.detail: Advances in AI are making it increasingly difficult to distinguish between real and fabricated content, raising concerns about the reliability of digital information and the potential to manipulate public opinion. Researchers are stepping up efforts to detect and contain AI-generated misinformation, including coordinated manipulation by AI swarms.

Science.headline: Eating ultra-processed foods may significantly raise the risk of serious heart problems
Science.detail: A major U.S. study found that people consuming around nine servings per day of ultra-processed foods like chips, frozen meals, sugary drinks, and packaged snacks had a 67% increased risk of serious heart problems. The study highlights the potential dangers of diets high in ultra-processed foods.

Science.headline: Vernal Equinox Occurs
Science.detail: The vernal equinox will occur on Friday, March 20, 2026, marking the first day of spring in the Northern Hemisphere and the first day of autumn in the Southern Hemisphere. During the equinox, the Sun is positioned directly above the Earth's equator, resulting in nearly equal lengths of day and night across the globe.

Health.headline: Ultra-processed foods linked to infertility in US women
Health.detail: A new study suggests a link between the consumption of ultra-processed foods and infertility in women in the United States.

Health.headline: Fracture risk calculator can identify more patients who could benefit from parathyroid surgery
Health.detail: A new fracture risk calculator has been developed to identify more patients who could benefit from parathyroid surgery.

Health.headline: Why 4 in 10 children in Papua New Guinea miss vaccinations
Health.detail: A report highlights the reasons why a significant number of children in Papua New Guinea are not receiving vaccinations.

Health.headline: The brain region associated with moral inconsistency
Health.detail: Research identifies the specific brain region that is associated with moral inconsistency in individuals.

Health.headline: Australian Private Health Insurers Are Buying Up GP Clinics.
Health.detail: Private health insurers in Australia are increasingly acquiring general practitioner clinics, raising concerns about potential conflicts of interest and impacts on patient care.

Sports.headline: Carrick admits to anger in management after Man Utd's Newcastle defeat
Sports.detail: Michael Carrick expressed his frustration with management following Manchester United's defeat by Newcastle. Barcelona thrashed Newcastle while Spurs offer a glimmer of hope.

Sports.headline: Jones defeats Venus Williams in Miami for WTA 1000 breakthrough
Sports.detail: Francesca Jones defeated Venus Williams in Miami for a WTA 1000 breakthrough. Wimbledon expansion plans get boost after high court judgment.

Sports.headline: Hodgkinson leads British medal hopes at world indoor championships
Sports.detail: Keely Hodgkinson is leading British medal hopes at the world indoor championships. Hodgkinson is targeting a first world title.

Sports.headline: First-place Einarson eyes semifinal berth at women's curling worlds after 8th win
Sports.detail: Kerri Einarson is closing in on a playoff berth at the women's world curling championship with an 8-3 win Thursday morning over South Korea. The event is held in Calgary.

Sports.headline: Tanvi Sharma beats Anmol Kharb in a battle of teenagers on a good day for Indian women's singles
Sports.detail: At the Orleans Masters badminton tournament, Tanvi Sharma defeated Anmol Kharb in a match between teenagers, marking a positive day for Indian women's singles competitors.

Entertainment.headline: Oscars 2026 Recap: Must-See Moments and What Didn't Make It to TV
Entertainment.detail: A recap of the 2026 Oscars ceremony, highlighting the must-see moments and behind-the-scenes happenings that viewers didn't get to witness on television.

Entertainment.headline: Chrishell Stause Reacts to Reports Chelsea Lazkani Is Also Leaving 'Selling Sunset' (Exclusive)
Entertainment.detail: Chrishell Stause shares her reaction to the news that Chelsea Lazkani may also be departing from the reality TV show 'Selling Sunset'.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Frankie Paul and Dakota Mortensen Fight Video Shows Flying Stools and Child's Alleged Injury
Entertainment.detail: A video surfaces of a fight between Taylor Frankie Paul and Dakota Mortensen, allegedly showing flying stools and a child's injury, leading to a domestic violence investigation.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Frankie Paul Domestic Violence Allegations 2026: What We Know About the 'Mormon Wives' Incident
Entertainment.detail: Details emerge about the domestic violence allegations surrounding Taylor Frankie Paul, the new face of 'The Bachelorette,' and her ex, Dakota Mortensen.

Entertainment.headline: Rapper Afroman wins lawsuit against police over mockery music videos
Entertainment.detail: Rapper Afroman wins a lawsuit against the police for using his music videos in a mocking manner, celebrating freedom of speech after the verdict.

Crime & Justice.headline: Illegal Aliens from Mexico Indicted for Conspiracy to Manufacture and Distribute Methamphetamine Related to a Clandestine Lab in Calaveras County, California
Crime & Justice.detail: Two illegal aliens from Mexico have been indicted for conspiracy to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine related to a clandestine lab in Calaveras County, California.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ford Motor Company and Borough of Ringwood to Perform Final Cleanup Targeting Groundwater at Ringwood Mines/Landfill Site in New Jersey
Crime & Justice.detail: Ford Motor Company and the Borough of Ringwood will perform a final cleanup targeting groundwater at the Ringwood Mines/Landfill Site in New Jersey.

Crime & Justice.headline: Tennessee Woman Sentenced to 40 Months in Prison for Conspiring to Create and Distribute Videos Depicting Monkey Torture and Mutilation
Crime & Justice.detail: A Tennessee woman has been sentenced to 40 months in prison for conspiring to create and distribute videos depicting monkey torture and mutilation.

Crime & Justice.headline: Five Members of Sex Trafficking Ring Sentenced to Nearly 120 Years in Prison
Crime & Justice.detail: Five members of a sex trafficking ring have been sentenced to nearly 120 years in prison.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Resolves Foreign Bribery Investigation with Balt SAS; Healthcare Executive and Sales Consultant Indicted in Alleged Years-Long Foreign Bribery Scheme
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department has resolved a foreign bribery investigation with Balt SAS, and a healthcare executive and sales consultant have been indicted in an alleged years-long foreign bribery scheme.

Education.headline: QC bestowed education governance award
Education.detail: Quezon City has received the Seal of Good Education Governance from the Synergeia Foundation for the second consecutive year, recognizing the city's efforts to improve basic education through various programs and policies. The award acknowledges the city's commitment to accessible, high-quality education and its effective management of education initiatives.

Education.headline: Daily AI Summary | AI in Education News | Latest AI Tools, EdTech Trends & Research
Education.detail: The rapid evolution of AI technology is continuously shaping the landscape of education, with new AI tools and EdTech trends emerging regularly, potentially enhancing teaching and learning. Concerns are also growing that AI tools may cause more harm than good, even as their adoption by teachers and students increases.

Education.headline: Early overseas study pathways opened for Vietnamese secondary students
Education.detail: The Canada – Vietnam Cultural Education Council (CVCEC) is strengthening cooperation with the Toronto District School Board (TDSB) to help Vietnamese students access Canada's public school system earlier. This initiative aims to create an educational bridge, enabling students to adapt to global learning environments and broaden academic options.

Education.headline: SIM Global Education Highlights Integrated Undergraduate Experience, Combining Academic Learning and Career Preparation
Education.detail: SIM Global Education (SIM GE) structures its undergraduate programs to combine academic learning with career preparation and student engagement, reflecting the evolving expectations of higher education. SIM GE partners with universities in the UK, US, and Australia to provide access to internationally recognized degree programs in Singapore.

Education.headline: IIT JAM Result Out 2026: Direct Link To Check Results And Download Scorecard
Education.detail: The IIT JAM (Joint Admission Test for Masters) results for 2026 have been released. Candidates can now check their results and download their scorecards through the direct link provided.

Lifestyle.headline: Zara & John Galliano are teaming up for a two-year partnership
Lifestyle.detail: Fashion powerhouse Zara is collaborating with designer John Galliano for a two-year partnership, bringing Galliano's avant-garde vision to a wider audience.

Lifestyle.headline: Wendy's launches March Madness menu with chance to win $100,000
Lifestyle.detail: Wendy's is celebrating March Madness with a special menu and a contest where customers have a chance to win $100,000.

Lifestyle.headline: Homeowners to Spend $522B on renovations in 2026 amid tight housing market
Lifestyle.detail: Despite a tight housing market, homeowners are projected to spend a staggering $522 billion on renovations in 2026, according to Fox Business.

Lifestyle.headline: Aldi recalls popular snack food over possible rodent hair contamination.
Lifestyle.detail: Aldi has issued a recall for one of its popular snack foods due to the potential presence of rodent hair, prompting concerns among consumers.

Lifestyle.headline: Costco enters fertility care with massive discounts for members through new healthcare partnerships.
Lifestyle.detail: Costco is expanding its healthcare offerings by partnering to provide members with significant discounts on fertility care services, making these treatments more accessible.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on the Iran war escalation: as Trump breaks things, who will pick up the pieces?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses the escalating Iran war and questions who will address the resulting issues as Trump disrupts the situation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: War's hunger toll
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial discusses how the conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran is widening and causing far-reaching repercussions, specifically mentioning the UN's World Food Programme's concerns.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Larijani's killing
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial remembers the late Larijani, describing him as one of Iran's most powerful figures, a thinker and a soldier.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: How new tech is helping seniors live better, more independent lives
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Leana S. Wen discusses how innovations and new technologies are assisting seniors in aging in place and maintaining better health for longer.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The languages that cannot be found.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece in the Indian Express discusses languages that are at the risk of extinction.

World.headline: Iran warns of 'zero restraint' if energy facilities attacked again
World.detail: Iran strikes Israeli oil refinery in northern city of Haifa as Tehran launches attacks on regional energy facilities, warning it will show 'zero restraint' if its own energy infrastructure is targeted further.

World.headline: US F-35 aircraft makes emergency landing after a combat mission over Iran
World.detail: A US F-35 fighter jet made an emergency landing after a combat mission over Iran; the pilot is in stable condition.

World.headline: Mexican military says 11 killed in raid targeting Sinaloa cartel leader
World.detail: During a raid targeting a Sinaloa cartel leader, the Mexican military reports 11 fatalities.

World.headline: Trump mocks Japan about Pearl Harbor in response to question about Iran war
World.detail: During a meeting with the Japanese Prime Minister, Donald Trump referenced Pearl Harbor when asked about the ongoing war with Iran.

World.headline: Israel says Haifa oil refinery hit in Iranian missile attack
World.detail: Israel has confirmed that a missile attack by Iran struck the oil refinery in Haifa.

Zimbabwe.headline: Sales representative steals from employer, makes false robbery report
Zimbabwe.detail: A sales representative allegedly stole from their employer and then falsely reported a robbery to cover up the crime.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe shifts focus from donor funding to private sector financing
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is reportedly changing its economic strategy to rely more on private sector investment rather than depending on donor funding.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZANU PF, state actors dominate rights abuses — ZPP
Zimbabwe.detail: A report indicates that ZANU PF and state actors are the primary perpetrators of human rights abuses in Zimbabwe.

Zimbabwe.headline: Gender, human rights commissions merger lawful: AG
Zimbabwe.detail: The Attorney General has stated that the merger of the Gender and Human Rights Commissions is lawful.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim moves to deepen trade ties with Uganda
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is taking steps to strengthen its trade relationships with Uganda.
